sections.paragraph:
- Original: τίθημι

- Transliteration: Tithemi

- Phonetic: tith'-ay-mee

- Definition:

1. to set, put, place

a. to place or lay

b. to put down, lay down

1. to bend down

2. to lay off or aside, to wear or carry no longer

3. to lay by, lay aside money

c. to set on (serve) something to eat or drink

d. to set forth, something to be explained by discourse

2. to make

a. to make (or set) for one's self or for one's use

3. to set, fix establish

a. to set forth

b. to establish, ordain

- Origin: a prolonged form of a primary theo theh'-o (which is used only as alternate in certain tenses)

- TDNT entry: 10:32,1

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: A prolonged form of a primary word θέω theō (which is used only as an alternate in certain tenses); to place (in the widest application literally and figuratively; properly in a passive or horizontal posture and thus different from G2476 which properly denotes an upright and active position while G2749 is properly reflexive and utterly prostrate): - + advise appoint bow commit conceive give X kneel down lay (aside down up) make ordain purpose put set (forth) settle sink down.
